Spatial ligand-receptor interaction inference in Visium breast cancer data

Jia Zhao

June 28, 2026

Packages

rm(list = ls())
library(rdist)
library(ggplot2)
library(geoR)
## --------------------------------------------------------------
##  Analysis of Geostatistical Data
##  For an Introduction to geoR go to http://www.leg.ufpr.br/geoR
##  geoR version 1.9-6 (built on 2025-08-29) is now loaded
## --------------------------------------------------------------
library(locfit)
## locfit 1.5-9.12   2025-03-05
library(stringr)
library(lattice)
library(CONCISE)

Load data

#### Here, we load gene-by-cell count matrix and location matrix of this dataset.

rawcount <- readRDS(file = "/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise_pkg_reproduce/prepare_inputs/rawcount.rds")
loc <- readRDS(file = "/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise_pkg_reproduce/prepare_inputs/loc.rds")

Construct ligand-receptor pairs

#### Load CellChat human database (from the package), and perform quality control for ligands and receptors based on sparsity

data(cellchatdb_human)
pairdb <- cellchatdb.human
pairdb <- InteractionDB(pairdb = pairdb,
                        rawcount = rawcount,
                        sparsity_threshold = 0.05)
## Total number of ligands = 532
## Total number of ligands after sparsity QC = 227
## Total number of receptors = 490
## Total number of receptors after sparsity QC = 295

Spatial ligand-receptor interaction inference with CONCISE

#### Run CONCISE for inferring spatial interactions

pairdb <- CONCISE(
  pairdb = pairdb,
  rawcount = rawcount,
  loc = loc,
  Wx_distance_threshold = 2.3)
## Creating spatial kernels...
##  marginal kernel 1
##  marginal kernel 2
##  marginal kernel 3
##  interaction kernel
## Done!
## Iterating over choices of kernels and estimating parameters of marginal distributions...
##  marginal kernel 1
##  marginal kernel 2
##  marginal kernel 3
## Done!
## Estimating parameters of the joint spatial process and performing statistical inference...
##  pre-calculation for parameter estimation
##  parameter estimation for each pair in pairdb
##  pre-calculation for statistical inference
##  terms not related to marginal kernels
##  terms related to only one marginal kernel
##  terms related to both two marginal kernels
##  statistical inference for each pair in pairdb
## Done!
P_value <- pairdb$p_value
P_value_adjust <- p.adjust(P_value, method = "BY")
pairdb$p_value_adjust <- P_value_adjust
res_sig_BY <- pairdb[pairdb$p_value_adjust < 0.05, ]
print(paste0("CONCISE reported ", dim(res_sig_BY)[1], " ligand-receptor interaction pairs."))
## [1] "CONCISE reported 119 ligand-receptor interaction pairs."
#### Save results

write.csv(pairdb, file = "/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise_pkg_reproduce/Visium_lri/res_concise_lri.csv")

Tumor boundary enrichment analysis

rm(list = ls())
library(rdist)
library(ggplot2)
library(Seurat)
## Attaching SeuratObject
## 'SeuratObject' was built under R 4.3.1 but the current version is
## 4.3.3; it is recomended that you reinstall 'SeuratObject' as the ABI
## for R may have changed
library(SPARK)
library(geoR)
library(locfit)
library(stringr)
set.seed(42)
#### Load results
res_sig <- read.csv("/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise_pkg_reproduce/Visium_lri/res_concise_lri.csv", row.names = 1)
P_value <- res_sig$p_value
P_value_adjust <- p.adjust(P_value, method = "BY")
res_sig$p_value_adjust <- P_value_adjust
res_sig <- res_sig[res_sig$p_value_adjust < 0.05, ]
#### define spatial regions for the enrichment analysis - surounding tumor / tumor, only neighboring area
load("/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/spacci/Examples/Visium_breast_cancer_block_a_section_1/data/Visium_breast_cancer_block_a_section_1_sp_obj.RData")
rawcount <- as.matrix(sp_obj@assays$Spatial@counts)
lib_size <- as.vector(apply(rawcount, 2, sum))
loc <- as.matrix(sp_obj@images$image@coordinates)
n <- length(lib_size)
anno <- read.csv("/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise/Examples/Visium_breast_cancer_block_a_section_1/data/Human_Breast_Cancer_Metadata.tsv", sep="\t")
rownames(anno) <- anno$ID
annonames <- intersect(rownames(anno), colnames(sp_obj))
anno <- anno[annonames, ]
anno_vec <- rep("NA", n)
names(anno_vec) <- colnames(sp_obj)
anno_vec[annonames] <- as.vector(anno$annot_type)
sp_obj@meta.data$anno <- anno_vec

anno_vec_v2 <- anno_vec
anno_vec_v2[anno_vec_v2 != "Tumor"] <- "Non-tumor"
sp_obj@meta.data$anno_v2 <- anno_vec_v2

region_1 <- "Non-tumor"
region_2 <- "Tumor"
D <- pdist(loc, metric = "euclidean", p = 2)
lval <- min(D[D > 0]) * 1
Wx <- exp(-D^2/(2*(lval^2)))
Wx[Wx >= 0.5] <- 1.
Wx[Wx < 0.5] <- 0.
df_loc_w <- data.frame(x = loc[,2], y = -loc[,1], w = Wx[1,])
Wx[as.vector(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_v2) != region_1, ] <- 0 # row: region 1
Wx[, as.vector(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_v2) != region_2] <- 0 # col: region 2

sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_1 <- "None"
sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_1[(rowSums(Wx) > 0) | (colSums(Wx) > 0)] <- "Tumor boundary"
SpatialDimPlot(sp_obj, group.by = "anno_region_1", pt.size.factor = 3.5)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the Seurat package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_2 <- "None"
sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_2[(colSums(Wx) == 0) & (rowSums(Wx) == 0)] <- "Other"

region_1 <- "Tumor boundary"
region_2 <- "Other"
#### Create shuffle matrix for the enrichment analysis
n_try <- 100
mat_shuffle <- matrix(0, nrow = n_try, ncol = n)
for (i in 1:n_try) {
  mat_shuffle[i, ] <- sample(1:n, n, replace = FALSE)
}
#### Enrichment analysis for ligands
gt_s <- lib_size * res_sig$gts_lbs_ratio[1]
n_sig <- dim(res_sig)[1]
y_trans <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n)
for (i in 1:n_sig) {
  g_name <- res_sig$ligand_organized_qc[i]
  g_name <- unlist(strsplit(g_name, "+", fixed = TRUE))

  gen_y <- rawcount[g_name, , drop = FALSE]
  gen_y <- as.vector(apply(gen_y, 2, sum))
  a_hat <- res_sig$a1_hat[i]

  mu_s <- gt_s * a_hat
  y_v <- gen_y - mu_s
  y_trans[i, ] <- y_v
}

idx_region <- which(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_1 == region_1)
mean_val <- apply(y_trans[, idx_region], 1, mean)
mean_shuffle <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n_try)
for (i in 1:n_try) {
  y_trans_tmp <- y_trans[, mat_shuffle[i, ]]
  mean_shuffle[, i] <- apply(y_trans_tmp[, idx_region], 1, mean)
}
enrichment_score_L_region1 <- (mean_val - rowMeans(mean_shuffle)) / apply(mean_shuffle, 1, sd)

idx_region <- which(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_2 == region_2)
mean_val <- apply(y_trans[, idx_region], 1, mean)
mean_shuffle <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n_try)
for (i in 1:n_try) {
  y_trans_tmp <- y_trans[, mat_shuffle[i, ]]
  mean_shuffle[, i] <- apply(y_trans_tmp[, idx_region], 1, mean)
}
enrichment_score_L_region2 <- (mean_val - rowMeans(mean_shuffle)) / apply(mean_shuffle, 1, sd)
#### Enrichment analysis for receptors
y_trans <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n)
for (i in 1:n_sig) {
  g_name <- res_sig$receptor_organized_qc[i]
  g_name <- unlist(strsplit(g_name, "+", fixed = TRUE))

  gen_y <- rawcount[g_name, , drop = FALSE]
  gen_y <- as.vector(apply(gen_y, 2, sum))
  a_hat <- res_sig$a2_hat[i]

  mu_s <- gt_s * a_hat
  y_v <- gen_y - mu_s
  y_trans[i, ] <- y_v
}

idx_region <- which(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_1 == region_1)
mean_val <- apply(y_trans[, idx_region], 1, mean)
mean_shuffle <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n_try)
for (i in 1:n_try) {
  y_trans_tmp <- y_trans[, mat_shuffle[i, ]]
  mean_shuffle[, i] <- apply(y_trans_tmp[, idx_region], 1, mean)
}
enrichment_score_R_region1 <- (mean_val - rowMeans(mean_shuffle)) / apply(mean_shuffle, 1, sd)

idx_region <- which(sp_obj@meta.data$anno_region_2 == region_2)
mean_val <- apply(y_trans[, idx_region], 1, mean)
mean_shuffle <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sig, ncol = n_try)
for (i in 1:n_try) {
  y_trans_tmp <- y_trans[, mat_shuffle[i, ]]
  mean_shuffle[, i] <- apply(y_trans_tmp[, idx_region], 1, mean)
}
enrichment_score_R_region2 <- (mean_val - rowMeans(mean_shuffle)) / apply(mean_shuffle, 1, sd)
#### Visualization
enrichment_vis <- matrix(c(enrichment_score_L_region1, enrichment_score_L_region2,
                           enrichment_score_R_region1, enrichment_score_R_region2), ncol = 4)
rownames(enrichment_vis) <- res_sig$interaction_name
colnames(enrichment_vis) <- c(paste0("L - ", region_1), paste0("L - ", region_2),
                              paste0("R - ", region_1), paste0("R - ", region_2))
enrichment_vis_all <- enrichment_vis

enrichment_vis_df <- as.data.frame(enrichment_vis)
enrichment_vis_df <- enrichment_vis_df[(enrichment_vis_df$`L - Tumor boundary` > 0) & (enrichment_vis_df$`R - Tumor boundary` > 0), ]
enrichment_vis_df <- enrichment_vis_df[(enrichment_vis_df$`L - Tumor boundary` > 1.645) | (enrichment_vis_df$`R - Tumor boundary` > 1.645), ]

key_pairs <- c("POSTN_ITGAV_ITGB3",
               "GAS6_AXL",
               "THY1_ITGAV_ITGB3",
               "CXCL12_CXCR4",
               "FN1_ITGA3_ITGB1",
               "COL1A2_ITGA3_ITGB1",
               "LAMB3_ITGA6_ITGB4",
               "JAG1_NOTCH4"
)
key_pairs <- rev(key_pairs)
enrichment_vis <- enrichment_vis_all[key_pairs, ]
enrichment_vis <- enrichment_vis[, c("L - Tumor boundary", "R - Tumor boundary")]

df_plot <- data.frame(value = c(enrichment_vis),
                      abs_value = abs(c(enrichment_vis)),
                      x = c(rep(1, dim(enrichment_vis)[1]), rep(2, dim(enrichment_vis)[1])),
                      y = rep(1:dim(enrichment_vis)[1], 2)
)
df_plot$group <- "none"
df_plot$group[df_plot$value >= 0] <- "Positive"
df_plot$group[df_plot$value < 0] <- "Negative"
p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = y, size = abs_value, color = group)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.7) +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "", limits = c("1","2"), labels = c("Ligand","Receptor")) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = "Ligand recetpr pairs", limits = as.character(1:dim(enrichment_vis)[1]), labels = rownames(enrichment_vis)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Positive" = "red", "Negative" = "blue")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )
p

Visualize ligand-receptor pairs

rm(list = ls())
library(rdist)
library(ggplot2)
library(Seurat)
library(SPARK)
library(geoR)
library(locfit)
library(stringr)
set.seed(42)
load("/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/spacci/Examples/Visium_breast_cancer_block_a_section_1/data/Visium_breast_cancer_block_a_section_1_sp_obj.RData")
rawcount <- as.matrix(sp_obj@assays$Spatial@counts)
lib_size <- as.vector(apply(rawcount, 2, sum))

cellchatdb <- read.csv("/gpfs/gibbs/pi/zhao/jz874/project/jiazhao/concise_pkg_reproduce/Visium_lri/res_concise_lri.csv", row.names = 1)
gt_s <- lib_size * cellchatdb$gts_lbs_ratio[1]

rownames(cellchatdb) <- as.vector(cellchatdb$interaction_name)
cellchatdb.res.tmp <- cellchatdb["POSTN_ITGAV_ITGB3", ]

l_name <- cellchatdb.res.tmp$ligand_organized_qc
r_name <- cellchatdb.res.tmp$receptor_organized_qc
a1_hat <- cellchatdb.res.tmp$a1_hat
a2_hat <- cellchatdb.res.tmp$a2_hat

l_name <- unlist(strsplit(l_name, "+", fixed = TRUE))
r_name <- unlist(strsplit(r_name, "+", fixed = TRUE))
gen_y1 <- rawcount[l_name, , drop = FALSE]
gen_y1 <- as.vector(apply(gen_y1, 2, sum))
gen_y2 <- rawcount[r_name, , drop = FALSE]
gen_y2 <- as.vector(apply(gen_y2, 2, sum))

mu1_s <- gt_s * a1_hat
mu2_s <- gt_s * a2_hat
y1_v <- gen_y1 - mu1_s
y2_v <- gen_y2 - mu2_s

sp_obj@meta.data$Ligand <- y1_v
sp_obj@meta.data$Receptor <- y2_v

cols <- c("#9E0142", "#D53E4F", "#F46D43", "#FDAE61", "#FEE08B", "#FFFFBF", "#E6F598", "#ABDDA4", "#66C2A5", "#3288BD", "#522081")
cols <- rev(cols)
pt_loc <- GetTissueCoordinates(sp_obj, image = Images(sp_obj)[1])

pt_df <- data.frame(x = pt_loc$x, y = pt_loc$y, val = y1_v)
p <- ggplot(pt_df, aes(x = y, y = -x, color = val)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = cols, name = "Ligand") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top"
  )
p

y2_v[y2_v > 8] <- 8
pt_df <- data.frame(x = pt_loc$x, y = pt_loc$y, val = y2_v)
p <- ggplot(pt_df, aes(x = y, y = -x, color = val)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = cols, name = "Receptor") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top"
  )
p
